title: Market Brief — Tuesday, April 14, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 20% (105/336), H200 at 21% (51/308), and B200 at 34% (161/161) as fresh supply enters the market but hasn't loosened conditions. H100 spot dropped 13.8% day-over-day to $1.54/hr while H200 surged 10% to $2.84/hr, signaling demand rotation toward next-gen silicon. B200 volatility at 97.1% annualized screams price discovery in an immature market.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 20% (105/336), H200 at 21% (51/308), and B200 at 34% (161/161) as fresh supply enters the market but hasn't loosened conditions. H100 spot dropped 13.8% day-over-day to $1.54/hr while H200 surged 10% to $2.84/hr, signaling demand rotation toward next-gen silicon. B200 volatility at 97.1% annualized screams price discovery in an immature market.

DeepSeek is burning cash. At $0.38/M output on V3.2 against a TCO-adjusted H100 inference cost of $5.61/M for Llama 70B class models, DeepSeek is selling at a loss of roughly $5/M tokens, subsidizing adoption. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at ultra-premium margins above 97%. Anthropic Haiku 4.5 at $5/M output runs tight but viable. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.35/hr with 97% annualized volatility and only 34% availability is overpriced relative to H100s delivering comparable inference at 65% lower cost per hour. Undervalued trade: lock in H100 capacity at $1.54/hr spot while utilization slides (down 9.7pp in 30 days) and arbitrage the $5.61 TCO against premium API pricing tiers yielding 90%+ margins.
bullets: 20% of H100 GPUs are available, keeping the market firmly in scarce territory with only 105 of 336 units free.
51 of 308 H200 GPUs are available at 21%, also scarce, while spot price jumped 10% overnight to $2.84/hr.
$0.38/M output makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest model tracked, roughly 210x less than OpenAI o3 Pro.
97.1% annualized B200 price volatility is nearly 5x that of H100s, reflecting an unstable early market.
$3.18/M tokens on B200 inference gives the best raw unit economics, beating H200 cost by 42%.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token pricing has fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
